title: Camps - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Camps 121 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community AIDS/HIV Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Families/Friends of People With Cancer Former Foster Children Foster Children Muscular Dystrophy Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADVENTURES WITHOUT LIMITS ADVENTURE TRIPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outdoor programs for children and adults with all ability levels. Programs include hiking, kayaking, canoeing, rafting, paddleboarding cross-country skiing, snowshoeing, rock climbing, and camping. Offers partner programs and open enrollment trips for those with little or no experience. PO Box #670 Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Age 6 and older Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Services run 7 days per week. Email (503) 359-2568 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASHLAND AERIAL ARTS SUMMER CAMPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers summer camps. Classes ranging from aerial silks, trapeze, bungee, tightrope, hula hoop, juggling, crafts and more. Eligibility: Ages 4-18 Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm Email (541) 382-4682 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAMP FIRE COLUMBIA CAMP NAMANU SUMMER CAMP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a summer camp featuring a variety of outdoor activities and programs, including hiking, swimming, arts and crafts, exploring nature, archery, overnight hikes, weaving, high ropes challenge courses, and more. 319 SW Washington Street Suite 1200 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Grades 2-12 Hours: June-August, hours vary by camp Email (971) 340-1608 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAMP KESEM CAMP KESEM UNIVERSITY OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a week-long summer camp for children affected by a parent’s cancer that offers peer-based emotional support. Camp activities include games, arts and crafts, sports, water activities, and outdoor recreation. The camp is staffed by screened and trained college students. Eligibility: Ages 6-18 who have lost a parent to cancer, have a parent undergoing cancer treatment, or whose parent is a cancer survivor. Eligible children may return to Camp Kesem more than once. Children with disabilities and special needs are considered on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 525-0719 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAMP MORROW SUMMER CAMPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers summer camps. 79551 Morrow Road Wamic, OR 97063 Eligibility: Grades 1-12 Hours: Friday, June 12, 2026-Friday, August 7, 2026 Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAMP ROSENBAUM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides week-long overnight camps.
